assertions.title: Current docs are reachable from the canonical spine
assertions.reason: README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md correctly route readers into docs/INDEX and the canonical module/flow inventories remain reachable there, but three retained top-level docs still lack top-of-file non-canonical/reference-only labeling, so out-of-spine docs are not yet consistently clear when opened directly from the repo surface.

steps.action: Follow the canonical reader path from README.md and repo-root ARCHITECTURE.md into docs/INDEX.md.
steps.expected: Canonical entrypoints should route readers into the docs spine.
steps.observed: README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md both point readers to docs/INDEX.md as the canonical documentation entrypoint/signpost.

steps.action: Inspect docs/INDEX.md for canonical reachability of core current docs.
steps.expected: Current canonical docs should be reachable from the spine, including module and flow inventories.
steps.observed: docs/INDEX.md defines the spine, links MODULE-INVENTORY.md and FLOW-INVENTORY.md, and classifies retained non-spine docs separately.

steps.action: Inspect the previously failing retained top-level docs and compare them with the spine classification.
steps.expected: Docs outside the spine should be clearly labeled as non-canonical/historical/reference-only.
steps.observed: docs/INDEX.md and docs/deprecated/INDEX.md now classify the three targeted docs as reference-only, but the files themselves still open without top-of-file non-canonical/reference-only banners.

steps.action: Sample other retained top-level docs that could still look canonical.
steps.expected: Other retained docs should show explicit banners if they are outside the canonical spine.
steps.observed: Sampled retained docs such as developer-guide.md, endpoint-inventory.md, migration-guide.md, ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md, and accounting-portal-frontend-engineer-handoff.md are explicitly bannered.

evidence: README.md:245-246 and ARCHITECTURE.md:5-9 still route readers into docs/INDEX.md, so the canonical spine entrypoints are intact.
docs/INDEX.md:5 declares that packets not reachable through the index are outside the canonical docs tree, and docs/INDEX.md:46 and :73 link the canonical module and flow inventories.

docs/INDEX.md:173-175 and docs/deprecated/INDEX.md:162-164 now classify docs/ACCOUNTING_PORTAL_SCOPE_GUARDRAIL.md, docs/AUDIT_TRAIL_OWNERSHIP.md, and docs/accounting-portal-endpoint-map.md as reference-only retained docs with canonical replacements.
docs/CONVENTIONS.md:123-125 requires retained legacy docs to carry a redirect/archive notice at the top and to be clearly labeled as non-canonical or superseded.

docs/ACCOUNTING_PORTAL_SCOPE_GUARDRAIL.md:1-5, docs/AUDIT_TRAIL_OWNERSHIP.md:1-6, and docs/accounting-portal-endpoint-map.md:1-5 still open as ordinary active docs without any top-of-file non-canonical/reference-only banner.

Other retained top-level docs sampled in this rerun are clearly bannered: developer-guide.md:3 (`NON-CANONICAL`), endpoint-inventory.md:3 (`REFERENCE ONLY`), migration-guide.md:3 (`RETIRED / REFERENCE ONLY`), ERP-DOD-BIBLE.md:3 (`NON-CANONICAL`), accounting-portal-frontend-engineer-handoff.md:3 (`NON-CANONICAL / REFERENCE ONLY`).

issues.issue: Indexed as reference-only, but still lacks an in-file non-canonical/reference-only banner.
Indexed as reference-only, but still lacks an in-file non-canonical/reference-only banner.
Indexed as reference-only, but still lacks an in-file non-canonical/reference-only banner.

summary: Re-tested VAL-DOCS-002 on the repo-static surface. The canonical spine entrypoints and inventory links are now reachable, and the targeted retained docs are classified as reference-only in the index and deprecated registry, but the three targeted top-level docs still lack top-of-file non-canonical/reference-only banners, so the assertion remains failed.
